what: Combine the v263 fused-path stride hints with REDUCE_BSN=16 on the split-K reduction path
why: v263 is the best recent benchmark candidate, and the 16x2112x7168 split-K shape still pays reduction overhead; shrinking the fp32 partial reduction tile may help that one hot large-K case without perturbing the stronger fused-path changes

what: Combine the v263 fused-path stride hints with exact MAX_KSPLIT on the split-K reduction kernel
why: the 16x2112x7168 split-K path currently pads the reduction width to the next power of two even though only 7 partials are real; using the exact reduction extent may save useless fp32 reduction work while keeping the stronger v263 fused-path improvements

what: Combine the v263 fused-path stride hints with REDUCE_BSN=16 and exact MAX_KSPLIT on the split-K reduction path
why: v269 and v270 isolate two ways to cut split-K reduction overhead on the 16x2112x7168 shape; combining the narrower tile with the exact reduction width may stack while leaving all non-split-K fused paths unchanged

what: Resubmit the exact v263 fused-path hint set as the next leaderboard confirmation candidate
why: v263 is the best recent benchmark result at 8.713us and outperforms the current leaderboard best for this branch by roughly 2.2% on fixed inputs, so it is the strongest candidate to spend the next scarce leaderboard slot on once quota opens

what: Submit the v269 split-K reduce-tile variant now that quota is open
why: v269 is the cleanest next experiment after the v263 baseline confirmation: it changes only REDUCE_BSN on the hot 16x2112x7168 split-K path while keeping the stronger fused-path hints intact

what: Use REDUCE_BSN=32 with exact v263 fused-path hints on the split-K reduction path
why: the current sweep covers reduction widths 64 and 16 only; 32 is the missing midpoint and may balance block count against per-block efficiency better for the 16x2112x7168 partial buffer
